AsValue.doc: Returns the question's current answer as a plain value (number, string, array, or empty). Compare to `empty` to test whether the question was answered. Prefer direct use (`if (Q == empty)`) over calling .AsValue() explicitly.

Set.doc: Equivalent to the `=` assignment operator. Pass `empty` to clear the answer. Prefer direct assignment (`QUESTION = value`) — use .Set() only when passing the question as a callback target or when the operator form is not syntactically available.

param.name: value
param.doc: New value for question, or empty to clear

SetTable.doc: Set the values of the table with the given name on the given slide to the values generated by the diagram returned by createCrossDiagram or createFixedCrossTable.

param.name: keepTableWidth
param.doc:
If true, the columnsare split into minor cols. This will keep the width of the table.
If false the columns are cloned, which will keep the size of cols, but table may then exceed the slide width.

param.name: keepTableHeight
param.doc:
If true, the rows are split into minor rows. This will keep the height of the table.
If false the last row is cloned, which will keep the size of rows, but table may then exceed the slide height.

SetTableBackgroundColor.doc: Set background color for all cells in the table using array of colors.

SetTableBackgroundColorPartially.doc: Set background color of some of cells in the table using array of colors.

param.name: coloredCells
param.doc: List of array objects in which describes the cell row, column and color specified by color (See Color_fromAny). E.g. {{1, 2, {255,0,0}}, {4, 5, new Color({255,0,0})}, {4, 5, "#FF0000"}}

SetTableData.doc: Set the values of the table with the given name on the given slide to the values given.

param.name: keepTableWidth
param.doc:
If true, the last col is split into minor cols. This will keep the width of the table.
If false the last col is cloned, which will keep the size of cols, but table may then exceed the slide width.

param.name: keepTableHeight
param.doc:
If true, the last row is split into minor rows. This will keep the height of the table.
If false the last row is cloned, which will keep the size of rows, but table may then exceed the slide height.

Question.doc: Base class for all question types

AsValue.doc: Returns the question's current answer as a plain value (number, string, array, or empty). Compare to `empty` to test whether the question was answered. Prefer direct use (`if (Q == empty)`) over calling .AsValue() explicitly.

Set.doc: Equivalent to the `=` assignment operator. Pass `empty` to clear the answer. Prefer direct assignment (`QUESTION = value`) — use .Set() only when passing the question as a callback target or when the operator form is not syntactically available.

param.name: value
param.doc: New value for question, or empty to clear

ToString.doc: The string representation of the object.

NumberOfActiveNotContacted.doc: Number of not started answer sheets which are related to active sample rules

NumberOfActiveSample.doc: Number of answer sheets that are related to existing sample rules and where these sample rules are active (you can deactivate sample rules in the CATI)

NumberOfAvailable.doc: Number of answer sheets (both created via samples, from import, as well as through recurring links) that have not yet been answered.

NumberOfAvailableActive.doc: Number of answer sheets which are still active for answering. In other words the available answer sheets minus any sheets that have failed (user does not want to answer)

NumberOfCompleted.doc: Number of answer sheets which were completed

NumberOfCompletedToday.doc: Number of answer sheets which were completed on current date (server time-zone)

NumberOfInterviewSucceeded.doc: Number of interviews carried out through CATI that were successful.

NumberOfNotContacted.doc: Number of answer sheets which have yet not been started

NumberOfPartly.doc: Specifies the number of sample respondents that have partly answered the questionnaire (means they have at least clicked the link to open their personal version of the questionnaire)

NumberOfSample.doc: Total number of answer sheets related to the questionnaire

CountAnswerIncluded.doc: Enables or disables the count total answers row in table diagrams. This row is a statistical row shown at the bottom of the table. The value of the row tells us how many answers were given to the question. This may be the same as the total number of respondents, but given that the question type is multi, it may also be that it was more than this, since each respondent then can give more than one answer to one question.

Direction.doc: Get/set the direction determines how the totals are calculated either vertical or horizontal, using Diagram_DirectionType_xxx constant.

IncludeTotal.doc: Enables or disables the total row which can be shown for table diagrams.

Median.doc: Calculate the value of the middle observation. In case of an even number of observations the median is the average of the two values on each side of the middle point. Note that the median is NOT calculated based on the categories, but using all data in the dcs.All empty values are ignored.

MedianText.doc: Get/set median text(Default text is Median).

ObjectTypeName.doc: The name of the type of object.

PctText.doc: Get/set value to DiagramParameter's PctText (Default text in English is "(pct)")

RemoveEmptyColumns.doc: Remove empty column in table diagram.

ShowMode.doc: The constants determine how the results are presented; as absolute numbers or as percentages or as both. Using Diagram_ShowModeType_xxx constant

ShowTotalAsSum.doc: Returns the total percentage values (should be sum of pct values OR always is 100%), which can be shown for table diagrams. The values tells us percentage of answers were given to the question. This may be the same as the total percentage values, but given that the question type is MULTI, it may also be that it was more than this, since each respondent then can give more than one answer to one question.

ShowTotalRowAsAbsolute.doc: Enable/Disable showing the total Row in absolute numbers, even though it is a pct column

Significance.doc: Enables or disables the significance row which can be shown for table diagrams.

SignificanceDifferenceText.doc: Get/set Significance difference text. Default text is "[+] and [-] = Significant difference of 95% or more".

SignificanceLevel.doc: Set SignificanceLevel. Default when not set is 1.96.

SignificanceText.doc: Get/set Significance text. Default text is Significance.

SortDataType.doc: Get/set which data should be sorted, using Diagram_SortDataType_xxx constant.

StDev.doc: Estimates standard deviation based on a sample.

StError.doc: Enables or disables the standard error row which can be shown for table diagrams.

TotalAnswerText.doc: Set the text for the total answers row, which is one of the statistical rows that can be shown for tables. If this function is not used then the label for the row will just be the default value Total answers.

TotalText.doc: Get/set the text for the total row, which is one of the statistical rows that can be shown for tables. If this function is not used then the label for the row will just be the default value "Total".

UseAxisName.doc: Sets diagrams to use axis name instead of axis text as labels for the diagram series names (basically identifying what a question concerns). If not set the default value is False, meaning that axis texts will be used.

Variance.doc: Estimates variance based on a data cache column’s values.

TextGridQuestion.doc: Text grid question. Rows accessed via `[rowIndex]`. Each row compares/assigns like a TextQuestion (string). `=` takes an array of strings (one per row), or `empty` to clear all.

AsValue.doc: Returns the question's current answer as a plain value (number, string, array, or empty). Compare to `empty` to test whether the question was answered. Prefer direct use (`if (Q == empty)`) over calling .AsValue() explicitly.

Set.doc: Equivalent to the `=` assignment operator. Pass `empty` to clear the answer. Prefer direct assignment (`QUESTION = value`) — use .Set() only when passing the question as a callback target or when the operator form is not syntactically available.

param.name: value
param.doc: New value for question, or empty to clear

ToString.doc: The string representation of the object.

IsChanged.doc: True if the value of the question has changed

ObjectTypeName.doc: The name of the type of object.

QasId.doc: Id of the qas. 0 if no stored data.

UserId.doc: Id of the user. 0 if no user or no stored data.

TextQuestion.doc: Text question. Compare and assign with a string.

AsValue.doc: Returns the question's current answer as a plain value (number, string, array, or empty). Compare to `empty` to test whether the question was answered. Prefer direct use (`if (Q == empty)`) over calling .AsValue() explicitly.

Set.doc: Equivalent to the `=` assignment operator. Pass `empty` to clear the answer. Prefer direct assignment (`QUESTION = value`) — use .Set() only when passing the question as a callback target or when the operator form is not syntactically available.

param.name: value
param.doc: New value for question, or empty to clear

ToString.doc: The string representation of the object.

IsChanged.doc: True if the value of the question has changed

ObjectTypeName.doc: The name of the type of object.

QasId.doc: Id of the qas. 0 if no stored data.

UserId.doc: Id of the user. 0 if no user or no stored data.

WhereExpFragment.doc: Pre-parsed expression fragment for use with DataCacheSpecification.EvalWhereExps. Build static sub-expressions once outside loops, then combine with operators and substitute per-iteration values using the replacement constructor to avoid repeated parsing overhead.

constructors.doc: Parse an expression string into a reusable fragment. Expensive — avoid calling inside loops. Build static sub-expressions once and reuse them via the replacement or operator constructors.

param.name: expression
param.doc: A single CGScript expression, e.g. "Project_Id == 42" or "Status == [4,5]". Do NOT include a where keyword or aggregation function here.

constructors.doc: Build a complete aggregation expression: 'selector where condition'. The result is what you add to the EvalWhereExps dictionary.

param.name: selector
param.doc: The aggregation function, e.g. "count()" or "average(Score)". "count()" is optimised internally.

param.name: whereExp
param.doc: The filter condition fragment, e.g. built from the operator constructor.

constructors.doc: Clone a fragment and substitute placeholder identifier names with concrete values. Use this to build a template fragment once outside a loop (e.g. "COL == [4,5]") and produce cheap per-iteration variants by replacing the placeholder with the actual column name.

param.name: fragment
param.doc: Template fragment containing placeholder identifier names to replace.

param.name: replacement
param.doc: Dictionary mapping placeholder names to replacement values, e.g. {"COL": "Evaluate_3"} or {"DATES": #2020-01-01#}.

constructors.doc: Combine two fragments with a binary operator (WHEREEXPFRAGMENT_AND, WHEREEXPFRAGMENT_OR, WHEREEXPFRAGMENT_PLUS, etc.).

param.name: operation
param.doc: Operator constant — one of WHEREEXPFRAGMENT_AND / OR / PLUS / MINUS / MULT / DIV.

AddDependency.doc: If you access another cell, you need to inform the engine about this, so it can determine which cells to execute in parallel

param.name: key
param.doc: Key in Dictionary that this expression depends on
